title: The Second Sunday of Easter
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Sermon: "The Second Sunday of Easter" | Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver
Date: April 29, 2019 | Preacher: Broderick Greer

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This is the Second Sunday of Easter, occurring within the Octave of Easter in the Christian calendar. The readings would follow the Revised Common Lectionary: Acts 4:32-35, Psalm 23, 1 John 1:1-2:2, and John 20:19-31 (the "Doubting Thomas" passage). The sermon falls in the Easter Season, a period of celebration extending 50 days until Pentecost, characterized by resurrection themes and the post-Easter appearances of Jesus to the disciples.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church in 2019 was in relative stability following the 2018 General Convention, though ongoing tensions existed around LGBTQ+ inclusion and orthodox theology. The Diocese of Colorado, under Bishop Robert J. Wright (who served until 2020), was navigating typical diocesan ministry. Saint John's Cathedral is the mother church of the diocese and would have been a focal point for cathedral liturgies and major diocesan events, though no specific extraordinary denominational crises were dominating headlines at this exact moment.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver in late April 2019 was experiencing typical spring weather patterns after winter. The city was focused on local politics and development issues. The Denver Broncos NFL draft had just occurred on April 25-27, 2019, which would have been a major focus for the sports-minded community. No major natural disasters or civic emergencies appear to have dominated the immediate week before April 29.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

Special Counsel Robert Mueller's investigation into Russian interference in the 2016 election remained in the national consciousness, with the Mueller Report having been submitted to the Justice Department in late March and controversies over its full public release still active in late April. President Trump continued his first term with ongoing political polarization. The 2020 presidential campaign was beginning to take shape with early Democratic primary activity, though the race remained early.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Sri Lanka was still in the immediate aftermath of the Easter Sunday bombings that occurred on April 21, 2019, killing over 250 people in coordinated attacks on churches and hotels—a tragedy that would have been freshly present in the consciousness of Christian congregations including Saint John's. The UK remained in Brexit negotiations and uncertainty under Prime Minister Theresa May, facing the original March 29 deadline extension. Trade tensions between the US and China continued under Trump administration tariffs.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture in late April 2019 was dominated by Marvel's "Avengers: Endgame," which had just been released April 26 and was breaking all box office records. The zeitgeist combined political polarization and fatigue with escapist entertainment. For a religious community, the proximity of the Easter bombings in Sri Lanka would have created a particular poignancy to Easter celebrations—joy tempered by awareness of Christian suffering abroad. The general mood was pre-summer optimism mixed with anxiety about the political future and international stability.
